testCases.title: The user should be able to register successfully when providing valid, new email and password
preconditions: User is on the Registration page
No account exists with the provided email address

steps.content: Enter a valid and unique email address (e.g., 'newuser@example.com') into the 'Email' field
Enter a strong password (meeting complexity requirements) into the 'Password' field
Enter the same strong password into the 'Confirm Password' field
steps.expected: Confirm Password field accepts input, masked

steps.content: Tap the 'Register' or 'Sign Up' button
steps.expected: Registration process initiates, loading indicator may appear

testCases.expectedResult: The user should be successfully registered and redirected to the dashboard or a 'Verify Email' screen, with a success message displayed.

testCases.title: The system should prevent registration when an email address is already registered
preconditions: User is on the Registration page
An account already exists with 'existinguser@example.com'

steps.content: Enter an already registered email address (e.g., 'existinguser@example.com') into the 'Email' field
Enter a valid password into the 'Password' field
Enter the same valid password into the 'Confirm Password' field
Tap the 'Register' or 'Sign Up' button

testCases.expectedResult: An error message indicating 'Email already registered' or 'An account with this email already exists' should be displayed, and the user should remain on the Registration page.

testCases.title: The system should prevent registration when required fields are empty
preconditions: User is on the Registration page

steps.content: Leave the 'Email' field empty
Leave the 'Password' field empty
Leave the 'Confirm Password' field empty
Tap the 'Register' or 'Sign Up' button

testCases.expectedResult: Error messages like 'Email is required' and 'Password is required' should be displayed for each empty field, and the user should remain on the Registration page.

testCases.title: The user should be able to register successfully when using an email address with a plus alias
preconditions: User is on the Registration page
No account exists with the provided email address (including alias)

steps.content: Enter a valid email address with a plus alias (e.g., 'user.name+alias@domain.co.uk') into the 'Email' field
Enter a valid password into the 'Password' field
Enter the same valid password into the 'Confirm Password' field
Tap the 'Register' or 'Sign Up' button

testCases.expectedResult: The user should be successfully registered and redirected, confirming the system handles email aliases correctly.

testCases.title: The user should be able to log in successfully when providing valid email and password
preconditions: User is on the Login page
An account ('testuser@example.com', 'ValidP@ssword1') is already registered

steps.content: Enter the registered email address ('testuser@example.com') into the 'Email' field
Enter the correct password ('ValidP@ssword1') into the 'Password' field
Tap the 'Login' button
steps.expected: Login process initiates, loading indicator may appear

testCases.expectedResult: The user should be successfully logged in and redirected to their dashboard within 3 seconds.

testCases.title: The system should display a generic error message when the user provides invalid credentials
preconditions: User is on the Login page
An account ('testuser@example.com') is registered

steps.content: Enter a registered email address (e.g., 'testuser@example.com') into the 'Email' field
Enter an incorrect password (e.g., 'WrongP@ssword') into the 'Password' field
Tap the 'Login' button

testCases.expectedResult: A generic error message like 'Invalid email or password' should be displayed, and the user should remain on the Login page.

testCases.title: The system should display a generic error message when the user attempts to log in with an unregistered email
preconditions: User is on the Login page
The email 'unregistered@example.com' is not associated with any account

steps.content: Enter an unregistered email address (e.g., 'unregistered@example.com') into the 'Email' field
Enter any password (e.g., 'SomePassword1') into the 'Password' field
Tap the 'Login' button

testCases.expectedResult: A generic error message like 'Invalid email or password' should be displayed, and the user should remain on the Login page.

testCases.title: The user account should be locked out after a specified number of consecutive failed login attempts
preconditions: User is on the Login page
An account ('lockout@example.com') is registered
System configured to lock account after X failed attempts (e.g., 5 attempts)

steps.content: Enter the registered email ('lockout@example.com')
Enter an incorrect password for X-1 consecutive times, tapping 'Login' after each attempt
steps.expected: Each attempt displays 'Invalid email or password' message

steps.content: Enter the incorrect password for the Xth time
Tap the 'Login' button

testCases.expectedResult: A message indicating the account is locked or guidance to use 'Forgot Password' should be displayed, and subsequent login attempts should fail until unlocked or reset.

testCases.title: The user should be able to register and log in via Google SSO as a new user
preconditions: User is on the Login/Registration page
User has a Google account
No existing account linked to the Google email

steps.content: Tap the 'Continue with Google' button
steps.expected: Redirects to Google authentication consent screen

steps.content: Select or enter Google account credentials
Grant required permissions on the Google consent screen

testCases.expectedResult: The user should be successfully registered (if new) and logged in, then redirected to the dashboard.

testCases.title: The user should be able to log in via Google SSO when an existing account has a matching email
preconditions: User is on the Login/Registration page
User has a Google account
An existing account ('existing@example.com') is registered via email/password, matching the Google account email

steps.content: Tap the 'Continue with Google' button
steps.expected: Redirects to Google authentication consent screen

steps.content: Select or enter Google account credentials for 'existing@example.com'
Grant required permissions on the Google consent screen

testCases.expectedResult: The user should be successfully logged into their existing account (possibly linking the Google SSO to it) and redirected to the dashboard.

testCases.title: The system should handle gracefully when the user cancels the Google SSO flow
preconditions: User is on the Login/Registration page

steps.content: Tap the 'Continue with Google' button
steps.expected: Redirects to Google authentication consent screen

steps.content: Tap the 'Cancel' or 'Back' button on the Google authentication screen

testCases.expectedResult: The user should be returned to the Login/Registration page in the application, without an error message (or a clear 'Login cancelled' message).

testCases.title: The system should handle gracefully when a network interruption occurs during Google SSO authentication
preconditions: User is on the Login/Registration page

steps.content: Tap the 'Continue with Google' button
steps.expected: Redirects to Google authentication consent screen

steps.content: Simulate network interruption (e.g., turn off Wi-Fi/data) while on the Google consent screen or during redirect back to the app
Restore network connection

testCases.expectedResult: The application should display an appropriate error message (e.g., 'Network error, please try again') and allow the user to retry the SSO flow or return to the Login/Registration page.

testCases.title: The user should be able to successfully reset their password via email
preconditions: User is on the Login page
An account ('resetuser@example.com') is registered

steps.content: Tap the 'Forgot Password?' link/button
Enter the registered email address ('resetuser@example.com') into the 'Email' field
Tap the 'Submit' or 'Send Reset Link' button
steps.expected: A confirmation message is displayed, and an email is sent

steps.content: Access the email inbox for 'resetuser@example.com' and click the password reset link
steps.expected: Redirects to a 'Set New Password' page in the app/browser

steps.content: Enter a new strong password into the 'New Password' field
steps.expected: New Password field accepts input, masked

steps.content: Enter the same new strong password into the 'Confirm New Password' field
steps.expected: Confirm New Password field accepts input, masked

steps.content: Tap the 'Reset Password' or 'Submit' button

testCases.expectedResult: The password should be successfully reset, and the user should be redirected to the Login page or dashboard with a success message. The user should then be able to log in with the new password.

testCases.title: The system should send a generic confirmation message when a password reset is requested for an unregistered email
preconditions: User is on the 'Forgot Password' screen
The email 'nonexistent@example.com' is not associated with any account

steps.content: Enter an unregistered email address (e.g., 'nonexistent@example.com') into the 'Email' field
Tap the 'Submit' or 'Send Reset Link' button

testCases.expectedResult: A generic confirmation message like 'If an account exists for this email, a password reset link has been sent' should be displayed to prevent email enumeration, and no email should be sent.

testCases.title: The user should not be able to reset the password using an expired or already used reset link
preconditions: User has initiated a 'Forgot Password' request and received a reset link
The reset link has either expired (due to time) or been used for a previous password reset

steps.content: Click the expired/used password reset link from the email
steps.expected: Redirects to a page in the app/browser

testCases.expectedResult: An error message indicating 'Link expired' or 'Link already used' should be displayed, and the user should be prompted to request a new password reset link.

testCases.title: The system should invalidate previous reset links when multiple 'Forgot Password' requests are made for the same email
preconditions: User is on the 'Forgot Password' screen
An account ('multiplerest@example.com') is registered

steps.content: Enter 'multiplerest@example.com' and tap 'Send Reset Link' (First request)
steps.expected: Confirmation message displayed, first reset email sent

steps.content: Wait a few seconds, then re-enter 'multiplerest@example.com' and tap 'Send Reset Link' (Second request)
steps.expected: Confirmation message displayed, second reset email sent

steps.content: Access the email inbox and click the link from the FIRST reset email
steps.expected: Redirects to a page in the app/browser

testCases.expectedResult: The link from the first email should be invalid, displaying an error like 'Link expired' or 'Link invalid'. Only the most recent reset link should be functional.

testCases.title: Verify the error message should display 'Invalid email format' when user enters email without '@' symbol
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Login page

steps.content: Enter an email without an '@' symbol (e.g., 'invalidemail.com') into the 'Email' field
Attempt to proceed (e.g., tap 'Register' or 'Login' or blur the field)

testCases.expectedResult: An inline error message 'Invalid email format' or similar should be displayed below the 'Email' field.

testCases.title: Verify the error message should display 'Invalid email format' when user enters email with trailing dot in domain
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Login page

steps.content: Enter an email with a trailing dot in the domain (e.g., 'user@domain.') into the 'Email' field
Attempt to proceed (e.g., tap 'Register' or 'Login' or blur the field)

testCases.expectedResult: An inline error message 'Invalid email format' or similar should be displayed below the 'Email' field.

testCases.title: Verify the system should display specific error messages when password does not meet complexity requirements during registration
preconditions: User is on the Registration page
Password complexity requires: min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 number, 1 special character

steps.content: Enter a valid email address
Enter a password that is too short (e.g., 'short')
Enter the same password in 'Confirm Password'
Tap 'Register'

testCases.expectedResult: An error message like 'Password must be at least 8 characters long' should be displayed. Repeat for other complexity rules (missing uppercase, number, special character) ensuring specific messages are shown for each.

testCases.title: The system should prevent registration when the password contains only whitespace characters
preconditions: User is on the Registration page

steps.content: Enter a valid email address
Enter multiple whitespace characters (e.g., '    ') into the 'Password' field
Enter the same whitespace characters into the 'Confirm Password' field
Tap the 'Register' or 'Sign Up' button

testCases.expectedResult: An error message like 'Password cannot be empty or contain only whitespace' or 'Password must meet complexity requirements' should be displayed, preventing registration.

testCases.title: The system should sanitize or escape input to prevent XSS attacks in email and password fields
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Login page

steps.content: Enter a potential XSS payload (e.g., '<script>alert("XSS")</script>') into the 'Email' field
Enter a valid password
Attempt to proceed (e.g., tap 'Register' or 'Login')
Observe client-side and server-side behavior (e.g., inspect page source, check server logs)

testCases.expectedResult: The XSS payload should be safely encoded/escaped, and no script execution should occur on the client or server side. Registration/Login might fail due to invalid format but no XSS should trigger.
